content.text: are the two main ways to manage Claude Code’s conversation context when a session gets long. Compacting keeps the important parts of the conversation in a shorter summary, while clearing throws the session away and starts fresh.

is the “smart compression” option. It summarizes the conversation so Claude can keep the key decisions, findings, and constraints while dropping a lot of the conversational noise. This is useful when you are still working on the same task but the session is getting too long and cluttered.

Typical uses:
- Preserve decisions from a planning session.
- Keep important architecture notes.
- Shrink a long research thread before continuing.
- Avoid losing progress when the context window is filling up.

is the hard reset. It deletes the conversation history so the next message starts with a clean slate. This is the right choice when you are done with one task and moving to something unrelated, or when the session has become too messy to salvage.

Typical uses:
- Switch from one feature to a completely different one.
- Remove confusing old context.
- Recover when the conversation has drifted too far from the current goal.
- Start over after a wrong direction has polluted the session.
Same task, but the session is long
You want to preserve key decisions
The context is too messy or poisoned

Claude Code performs best when the context stays focused. If too much irrelevant history builds up, the model can repeat itself, drift off task, or lose precision. Using compact and clear intentionally helps keep the workflow sharp instead of waiting for automatic compaction to happen at a bad moment.
as cleaning a crowded desk by stacking the important papers into a neat folder, and
as emptying the desk completely before starting a new project.
preserves important information in a shorter summary.
removes the old session entirely and starts fresh.
Use compact when the same task continues; use clear when the task changes.
Managing context deliberately helps Claude stay accurate and focused.
